_comment: Post-apply UX smoke tests. Run after every module apply to catch hidden-UX-dep regressions where a service change silently breaks a Windows UX flow. Each test names the flow, a probe (service-state check, ms-settings URI, or PS check), the services that must be running/enabled for it to work, and remediation. If a probe fails, the orchestrator points at the last module that changed any of the listed services. See 2026-07-07 BT pairing incident in knowledge_base.

tests.flow: Settings > Bluetooth & devices > Add device > Bluetooth (discovery)

probe.note: Full end-to-end probe would require launching ms-settings:bluetooth and inspecting the wizard, which we can't automate reliably. Service-state check is the closest deterministic signal.

tests.onFailure: The Windows 11 'Add device' Bluetooth wizard will silently close on click. Enable the required services (Set-Service <name> -StartupType Manual; Start-Service <name>). Restart bthserv.

tests.flow: Settings > Printers & scanners > Add device - network discovery back-end
tests.note: Only checks the shared-discovery back-end. Spooler being Disabled is legitimate when user answered NO to printing in Q1. If user prints, the printer_scanner category correctly avoids disabling Spooler.
tests.onFailure: Network printer discovery in the Add printer wizard is broken. fdPHost/FDResPub are tripwire - if Disabled, something bypassed the tripwire enforcement. Restore to Manual.

probe.note: Launch the URI and check within timeout whether SystemSettings.exe is running. If URI fires but process doesn't start, the Settings UWP stack is broken (Appx package missing or dependency service disabled).

tests.onFailure: Settings app deep-links silently fail. Check AppXSvc + StateRepository + TokenBroker. If services are fine, run: Get-AppxPackage -AllUsers Microsoft.Windows.ImmersiveControlPanel - if missing, restore via DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth then re-register with Add-AppxPackage.

tests.note: Skipped as FAIL when user explicitly answered YES to Q12 (turn off WSearch). Orchestrator drops this test from the run in that case. Kept in the suite because most users don't disable WSearch and a silent regression is bad.
tests.onFailure: Start menu returns 'no results' or takes 30+ seconds. Re-enable WSearch (only disable if user explicitly answered yes to Q12 AND has 'Everything' installed).

tests.flow: Any Teams / Slack / OneDrive notification arrives
tests.onFailure: Toast notifications silently drop. Re-enable WpnService.

tests.flow: Sign in to Microsoft account in Settings / Store / OneDrive
tests.onFailure: Sign-in dialog either doesn't appear or fails with generic error. Re-enable TokenBroker + wlidsvc.

tests.flow: Any UWP / Store app launches (Photos, Calc, Store, etc.)
tests.onFailure: Store apps refuse to open or immediately close. Re-enable all listed services to Manual.

tests.flow: Switch audio output from taskbar volume flyout
tests.onFailure: Audio device list is empty or won't switch. AudioSrv/AudioEndpointBuilder are tripwire - investigate why they're not running.

tests.flow: Recycle Bin is intact after storage module
tests.note: Added 2026-07-07 after audit. Storage module previously deleted C:\$Recycle.Bin regardless of riskLevel schema. Now gated behind confirmed:true; this test verifies the folder still exists after any storage apply.
tests.onFailure: Recycle Bin storage was destroyed. If storage module ran, check apply.log for a $Recycle.Bin source that was not confirmed by the user.

tests.flow: Windows.old rollback available (if it was ever present)
tests.note: Not a failure if Windows.old never existed on this machine. FAIL means storage module deleted a Windows.old that existed at diagnose time.
tests.onFailure: Windows.old (feature-update rollback data) was deleted. Ensure the storage plan entry for windows_old has confirmed:true only if the user explicitly agreed.

tests.flow: Lid-close power button behavior is a real setting (not silently forced to Do Nothing)
tests.note: Just checks that LIDACTION is not 0 for BOTH AC and DC (Do Nothing on both is the pc-cleaner default that surprised users). If the user actually wants that, they can override; this smoke just flags the silent-apply case for post-run review.
tests.onFailure: Lid-close is set to Do Nothing on both AC and battery. If you didn't explicitly ask for this, revert the power module apply.

tests.flow: DNS resolution works for a common host after network module
tests.note: Runs Resolve-DnsName on a well-known host. FAIL means the DNS override targeted an unreachable server, the network profile is captive-portal and pc-cleaner overrode it anyway, or the VPN was disrupted.
tests.onFailure: DNS resolution failed. If network module ran with DNS override, revert. If not, check WiFi/VPN state.

tests.flow: iPhone / iPad appears in iTunes when plugged in via USB
tests.note: Added 2026-07-08 after incident. Only runs if Apple software is installed on the machine (services present) — skipped otherwise. Checks that the three services backing the iOS-over-USB path are not disabled. If any are Disabled, iPhone will not appear in iTunes / Explorer even with the phone unlocked and Trust prompt accepted.
tests.onFailure: iPhone / iPad silently won't appear in iTunes. Restore: Set-Service 'Apple Mobile Device Service' -StartupType Automatic; Set-Service StiSvc -StartupType Automatic; Set-Service 'Bonjour Service' -StartupType Automatic; then Start-Service on each. All three are tripwire — if any is Disabled, something bypassed the tripwire enforcement (check apply.log for BLOCK entries, or a pre-tripwire snapshot state).
